title: Nephrodium odontoloma
intro: Nephrodium odontoloma, also known as the Toothed Nephrodium or the Toothed Shield Fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium odontoloma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium odontoloma is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Nephrodium odontoloma is classified as a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Nephrodium, which is now considered to be a synonym of the genus Phanerophlebia. The family Matoniaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by their simple, lobed or divided leaves. The classification of Nephrodium odontoloma has undergone changes over time, with some sources grouping it under the genus Phanerophlebia or other related ferns. The exact classification of this species can be complex and may vary depending on the source or recent taxonomic revisions.
